name: Arlington Community
description_short: Arlington Community Federal Credit Union is a member-owned credit union serving Virginia since 1951, offering personal and business banking with competitive rates and community-focused service.
description_long:
Arlington Community Federal Credit Union (ACFCU) has been operating as a not-for-profit, member-owned financial cooperative since 1951. The credit union is NCUA-insured and serves individuals and businesses in Virginia with a commitment to financial empowerment and community engagement. The organization emphasizes remarkable service and positions itself as a cooperative where members benefit collectively from their participation. As of the website content, ACFCU announced plans to merge with CommonWealth One Federal Credit Union to create a stronger combined entity.

ACFCU offers a comprehensive suite of personal financial products including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit, retirement accounts, mortgages, home equity lines of credit, vehicle loans, credit cards, and personal loans. For business members, the credit union provides business checking and savings, business loans, business lines of credit, commercial mortgages, and SBA loans. The organization also offers investment services, insurance coverage, and mobile and online banking access. Financial wellness services include free financial education webinars, financial counseling, credit score tracking through SavvyMoney, and a learning hub with calculators and FAQs.

What distinguishes ACFCU is its emphasis on member rewards and personalized service rather than profit extraction. The credit union advertises competitive certificate rates (3.90% APY on 18-month certificates at time of website snapshot) and same low rates for new or used vehicle loans with flexible terms and fast approvals. The organization explicitly commits to community involvement and staff volunteerism, positioning itself as deeply embedded in Arlington rather than a corporate entity. The credit union's routing number is 256078404, and it provides ATM and branch locator services plus fraud prevention resources.

As a credit union, ACFCU is member-owned and operates on a not-for-profit basis, which typically results in better rates and lower fees than for-profit banks. However, the website does not provide specific fee schedules, rate details, or membership eligibility requirements, making it difficult to compare concrete offerings without direct contact. The pending merger with CommonWealth One may affect current member experiences and product offerings during transition. As with all credit unions, membership requirements or field-of-membership restrictions may apply depending on eligibility criteria not detailed on the public website.
services: Personal checking accounts with cash back rewards
Personal savings accounts and certificates of deposit
Retirement savings accounts (IRAs and qualified plans)
Mortgage lending for home purchase and refinance
Vehicle loans (new and used) with flexible terms
Business loans and business lines of credit
pros: Not-for-profit, member-owned structure typically offers better rates and lower fees than for-profit banks
Competitive certificate rates advertised (3.90% APY on 18-month CDs)
Same low rates for both new and used vehicle loans with flexible terms
Comprehensive product suite including personal, business, mortgage, and investment services
Free financial education webinars and financial counseling services
Mobile and online banking with SavvyMoney credit score tracking included
Long operational history since 1951 with established community ties in Virginia
Cash back rewards checking account for everyday purchases and major life events
cons: Website does not disclose specific membership eligibility requirements or field-of-membership restrictions
No detailed fee schedule or rate comparison information provided on public website
Pending merger with CommonWealth One creates uncertainty about service continuity and product changes
Limited information about minimum account balances, deposit requirements, or loan qualification criteria
Geographic limitation to Virginia membership base may restrict access for out-of-state consumers
best_for: Virginia residents seeking competitive rates on mortgages, vehicle loans, and savings products
Small business owners looking for SBA loans and commercial lending with community-oriented service
Consumers prioritizing community involvement and member-owned cooperative financial services over corporate banking
Individuals wanting comprehensive financial wellness resources including free counseling and educational webinars
diagnosis: Arlington Community FCU is best for Virginia residents and business owners who are eligible for membership and prioritize member-owned, not-for-profit financial services with community engagement. The main caveat is that specific rates, fees, and membership eligibility criteria are not publicly disclosed on the website, requiring direct contact to compare offerings against competitors, and the announced merger with CommonWealth One introduces transition uncertainty.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: Arlington Community — NCUA-insured credit union in Arlington, VA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
